description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build-org, filter-css-classes, and publish. In the build-org stage add an org-generation job using the iquiw/alpine-emacs image; before the script run apk update, apk add graphviz ttf-freefont, and copy an existing elpa directory if present, cache the .timestamps and elpa/ directories under key org-files, then execute "emacs -batch -q -l export.el -f org-publish-with-different-timestamp-directory" followed by "cp -rf /root/.emacs.d/elpa/ ." and store the generated _posts and res directories as artifacts. In the filter-css-classes stage add a css-theme job using node:6.11.3-alpine; before the script set npm cache to $(pwd)/.npm_cache, cd into syntax-extractor and run npm install, cache the .npm_cache directory with key node-libraries, then run "node syntax-extractor/index.js base16-default-oceanic" and publish the resulting _assets/css/syntax.scss as an artifact. In the publish stage add a pages job using ruby:2.7.4-alpine3.14 that runs only on the master branch; before the script update apk, install build-base, and run "bundle install --path .bundle_cache/", cache .bundle_cache with key jekyll, then build the site with "JEKYLL_ENV=production bundle exec jekyll build -d public" and expose the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact.
